description: Implement maximum size constraints (A4-equivalent) on markdown cards with internal vertical scrolling, while preserving natural auto-sizing for smaller content.
steps: Step 1: Update getNodeDimensions function (src/App.jsx, line 508) to increase the maximum card width to approximately 600px (A4-equivalent width on canvas). The current cap is Math.min(380, width). Change this to approximately 600. Also add a height calculation that grows naturally but caps at approximately 800px (A4-equivalent height). Return both width and height from the function.

Step 2: Update the card outer container div (src/App.jsx, around line 4748) to apply the max-height constraint. Add maxHeight to the style object using nodeDims.height or a fixed MAX_CARD_HEIGHT constant (approximately 800px). Ensure overflow is hidden at this level so the card itself does not grow beyond the max.

Step 3: Update the content area wrapper div (src/App.jsx, around line 4860) - the div with className containing 'mt-2 flex-1' - to add max-height and overflow-y:auto so content scrolls internally when it exceeds available space. Use a CSS class or inline style like 'overflow-y: auto; max-height: calc(100% - title area)'. The simplest approach: add 'overflow-y-auto' and 'max-h-[calc(800px-80px)]' or similar to the content div, or use flex-1 with overflow-y-auto (since the parent has a fixed max-height, flex-1 + overflow-y-auto will handle it).

Step 4: Add CSS for custom-scrollbar styling in src/index.css. The class 'custom-scrollbar' is already referenced in App.jsx but not defined. Add a thin, subtle scrollbar style for webkit and Firefox: thin scrollbar with slate/transparent track.

Step 5: Ensure word-wrap behavior prevents horizontal scrolling. In the markdown content container and MarkdownRenderer, add word-wrap/overflow-wrap: break-word. Check the existing .markdown-content pre style in index.css - it already has overflow-x:auto and max-width:100% which is good for code blocks. Add 'break-words' (Tailwind) or 'word-break: break-word' to the content container to prevent horizontal overflow of long text.

Step 6: Define max card dimension constants at the top of App.jsx (near MARKDOWN_ZOOM_THRESHOLD on line 336) for maintainability: const MAX_CARD_WIDTH = 600; const MAX_CARD_HEIGHT = 800; Use these in getNodeDimensions and the card container style.
acceptance_criteria: Small cards with little content remain compact and auto-sized (no change in behavior)
Cards with moderate content expand naturally up to the maximum dimensions
Cards never exceed approximately 600px width and 800px height (A4-equivalent)
Content that exceeds the maximum height becomes internally scrollable with vertical scrollbar
Long text lines wrap within the card width - no horizontal scrollbar on text content
The custom-scrollbar class provides a subtle, thin scrollbar appearance
npm run build succeeds without errors
verification: Run 'npm run build' and confirm it succeeds without errors
Verify MAX_CARD_WIDTH and MAX_CARD_HEIGHT constants exist near line 336 in App.jsx
Verify getNodeDimensions returns both width and height with proper caps
Verify card container div applies maxHeight from the dimensions
Verify content area has overflow-y-auto for internal scrolling
Verify custom-scrollbar CSS class is defined in index.css
findings: Implementation complete. Build passes. Constants MAX_CARD_WIDTH=600 and MAX_CARD_HEIGHT=800 defined at line 336. getNodeDimensions now returns {width, height} with both capped. Card container applies maxHeight+overflow:hidden. Content div conditionally uses overflow-y-auto and custom-scrollbar only when nodeDims.height >= MAX_CARD_HEIGHT; smaller cards use overflow-hidden with no maxHeight constraint. Custom-scrollbar CSS defined in index.css. Word-break applied via both Tailwind class and inline styles. Bug fix applied: scrollbars no longer appear on small/medium cards.
